title: Bendakaya Roti Pachadi
description: A classic Andhra-style okra chutney made with tamarind and finished with aromatic tempering. Best enjoyed with hot rice and ghee or soft chapati.

tips: Ensure okra is completely dry before cooking to prevent sliminess.
subSteps: Wash okra thoroughly and pat completely dry with a cloth.
Chop okra into small 1/4 inch pieces.
Chop onion into small pieces.
Slit green chillies lengthwise.
Peel and lightly crush garlic cloves.
Soak tamarind in warm water for 5 minutes and extract thick pulp.

ingredients.name: Oil
ingredients.notes: 2 tbsp for cooking, 1 tbsp for tempering

ingredients.name: Cumin Seeds
ingredients.notes: 1 tsp in chutney, 0.5 tsp in tempering

steps.instruction: Cook okra until dry and non-sticky.
tips: Do not cover the pan, moisture will make okra sticky.
Cook until it looks slightly crisp and separated.
subSteps: Heat 2 tbsp oil in a pan on medium flame.
Add chopped okra and sauté uncovered.
Cook for 12–15 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Okra will first release moisture, then turn dry and non-sticky.
Ensure it is fully cooked and slightly crisp.
Turn off the heat and add green chillies, garlic, and onion.
Mix well with residual heat until onions soften slightly.
Allow mixture to cool completely.
Transfer to mixer jar.
Add tamarind pulp and salt.
Grind to coarse or smooth consistency without adding water.
Transfer to serving bowl.
Heat 1 tbsp oil in a small pan.
Add mustard seeds and let them splutter.
Add cumin seeds and sauté for 20–30 seconds.
Add dry red chillies and curry leaves.
Add crushed garlic and sauté until golden and fragrant.
Pour hot tempering over chutney.
Mix well before serving.

seo.metaTitle: Bendakaya Roti Pachadi Recipe | Andhra Okra Chutney
seo.metaDescription: Authentic Andhra bendakaya roti pachadi made with okra, tamarind, and tempering. Simple, flavorful chutney for rice or chapati.
